questions.question: Apply the FOIL algorithm to learn a first-order rule for predicting the outcome of a movie based on its genre and release year. If the movie is a comedy and was released before 2000, what is the predicted outcome? Use the rule in the form 'p -> q' where p is the formula representing the conditions and q is the outcome. You can assume the following probabilities: P(comedy) = 0.4, P(release before 2000) = 0.7, P(comedy and release before 2000) = 0.28. Write your answer in the required format.

A student wants to learn a first-order rule for predicting the grade of a student based on their attendance and hours studied. If the student has attended at least 80% of classes and has studied for 15 hours or more, what is the predicted grade? Use the FOIL algorithm to induce a rule from the following dataset:

| Attendance | Hours Studied | Grade |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1.0 | 10 | A |
| 1.0 | 20 | B |
| 1.0 | 5 | F |
| 0.5 | 15 | C |
| 0.5 | 10 | D |
| 0.5 | 5 | F |
| 1.0 | 10 | A |
| 0.8 | 20 | B |
| 0.8 | 15 | B |
| 0.8 | 5 | F |
| 1.0 | 10 | A |
| 0.9 | 20 | B |
| 0.9 | 15 | B |
| 0.9 | 5 | F |
| 1.0 | 10 | A |
| 0.8 | 20 | B |
| 0.8 | 15 | B |
| 0.8 | 5 | F |

A person wants to learn a first-order rule for predicting whether a person is a smoker based on their age and gender. Use the FOIL algorithm to induce a rule from the following dataset:

| Age | Gender | Smoker |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 20 | Male | No |
| 25 | Male | Yes |
| 30 | Male | Yes |
| 35 | Male | Yes |
| 20 | Female | Yes |
| 25 | Female | No |
| 30 | Female | No |
| 35 | Female | No |
| 20 | Male | No |
| 25 | Male | Yes |
| 30 | Male | Yes |
| 35 | Male | Yes |
